compiled_state.title: [GREEN] Implement validate subcommand in ticket CLI dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Implement the `validate` subcommand in the ticket CLI dispatcher and ticket-lib-api.sh library, turning the RED test from task 6116-bf82 GREEN.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (modify — add validate case to dispatcher + update _usage() string)
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh (modify — add ticket_validate() function)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ticket_validate()` added to plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh as a bare delegation: `bash "$_TICKETLIB_DIR/validate-issues.sh" "$@"; return $?` (no subshell wrapping with set -e — SOURCEABILITY CONTRACT must be preserved)
- [ ] `ticket_validate()` follows the ticket_transition thin-wrapper pattern exactly — function body is just the bash delegation, no subshell with set -euo pipefail around it
- [ ] `validate)` case added to plugins/dso/scripts/ticket dispatcher after the `untag)` case (before `*)`), using the standard pattern: `_ensure_initialized && source ticket-lib-api.sh && _ticketlib_dispatch ticket_validate "$@" && exit $?`
- [ ] _usage() string in dispatcher (lines ~29-44) updated to include `validate` with description "Validate ticket system health (--quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse)"
- [ ] Exit codes 0-4 from validate-issues.sh pass through the full dispatcher chain unmodified: validate-issues.sh exit → bash call exit → function return → _ticketlib_dispatch subshell exit → `exit $?` in dispatcher
- [ ] All flags (--quick, --full, --fix, --json, --terse, --verbose) pass through via "$@"
- [ ] validate-issues.sh uses TICKET_CMD env var to call back into ticket CLI — this must remain functional (do not hardcode TICKET_CMD)
- [ ] The SOURCEABILITY CONTRACT in ticket-lib-api.sh is preserved (no file-scope set -e, exit, or trap added)
- [ ] _TICKETLIB_DIR variable is used for the script path (not hardcoded plugins/dso/scripts/)
- [ ] Task 6116-bf82 RED test suite turns GREEN after this implementation
- [ ] validate-issues.sh is NOT modified (scope boundary)

## Notes
_ticketlib_dispatch wraps the function call in `( "$op" "$@" )` subshell. The function's return code becomes the subshell exit code. The dispatcher then does `exit $?`. This chain preserves the 5-tier exit code as long as ticket_validate() itself uses `bash "$_TICKETLIB_DIR/validate-issues.sh" "$@"; return $?` (bare delegation — not `exec` which would prevent the return $? syntax).
